doc_title: Churn Prediction with Sequential Data and Deep Neural Networks A Comparative Analysis*

items.fact: Verbeke et al. (2012) provide an extensive benchmark of classification techniques using several real-life data sets.
items.evidence_span: Verbeke et al. (2012) provide an extensive benchmark of classification techniques using several real-life data sets

items.fact: van den Poel and Larivière (2004) discuss survival analysis for churn modeling.
items.evidence_span: survival analysis (van den Poel and Larivière (2004))

items.fact: Wei and Chiu (2002) discuss aggregation procedures for time-varying features.
Song et al. (2006) discuss aggregation procedures for time-varying features.
Chen et al. (2012) propose a hierarchical multiple kernel support vector machine for churn prediction.
Martins (2017) compares the performance of LSTM with aggregated information using random forest.
Tan et al. (2018) propose a network architecture combining CNN and LSTM networks.
Wangperawong et al. (2016) process time-varying features as images and apply a CNN architecture.
Zaratiegui et al. (2015) encode sequential information as images and apply a CNN.
Zhou et al. (2019) combine different network architectures to leverage sequential data.
